summary: Linked the common fighter BarrelWait unit by matching `ftCo_8009EC70` and switching `ftCo_BarrelWait.c` from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`. The fix was primarily a stack/layout-sensitive decomp adjustment: replacing ad-hoc zeroed stack arrays with `PAD_STACK`, using precise float literals, exposing `ftCo_8009EC70` in the header, and introducing a temporary `SmallerHitCapsule` fake struct to model only the accessed hit-capsule fields while preserving stack/object layout. decomp.dev reported one new match: `ftCo_8009EC70` moved from 99.94% to 100.00%, with +308 matched bytes and +652 linked bytes.
what_changed: `ftCo_8009EB18` now gets the fighter with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` instead of directly using `gobj->user_data`, uses `PAD_STACK(4)`, and passes explicit float constants `0.0f`, `1.0f`, `0.0f` to `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`.
Removed the local static forward declaration for `ftCo_8009EC70` from the C file and added a public declaration for it to `ftCo_BarrelWait.h`.
`ftCo_BarrelWait.h` switched its forward include from `ftCommon/forward.h` to `ft/forward.h`, presumably to obtain the needed forward declarations for the exported function signature.
`ftCo_8009EC44` replaced a dummy zeroed stack array with `PAD_STACK(4)` before calling `Ground_801C4DA0`.

`ftCo_8009EC70` was rewritten with explicit inner scopes, a temporary `struct SmallerHitCapsule`, casts to `HitCapsule*` for `lbColl_80008D30` and `ftColl_80079EA8`, and `PAD_STACK(4)` near the stack-local hit capsule.

`ftCo_8009EC70` preserved the existing gameplay logic: set bury timer and position, update collision and camera, load hit-capsule collision data, refresh fighter collision, normalize negative knockback angles, mirror the angle based on facing direction, write damage fields, call `Fighter_UnkTakeDamage_8006CC30`, and finish with `ftCo_8008DCE0(gobj, 91, 0)`.
smart_moves: Used `PAD_STACK(4)` instead of fake zero-initialized byte arrays to express stack padding directly and make the matching intent clearer.
Introduced a narrowly scoped fake `SmallerHitCapsule` with a `@todo` note rather than pretending the real `HitCapsule` size/layout was fully known.
Placed the fake hit-capsule local inside an inner block, likely to force the desired stack lifetime/layout for matching.
Kept external APIs typed as `HitCapsule*` via casts while allowing the local stack object to use the smaller temporary layout needed by the decomp.
Changed integer-looking float arguments and comparisons to explicit float literals, which can matter for matching generated code and documents the intended types.
Moved `ftCo_8009EC70` to the header when the unit became linked, making the symbol available outside the C file instead of keeping it as a local-only declaration.
decomp_lessons: When a unit is nearly matching, stack object size can be the last blocker; a temporary fake struct with documented unknown padding may be preferable to misusing the presumed full struct.
Prefer `PAD_STACK(n)` over anonymous `u8 _[n] = { 0 }` padding when the bytes are only present to reproduce stack layout.
Use explicit float constants in motion-state and arithmetic-sensitive calls to avoid accidental integer constant codegen differences.
Inner scopes can be a valid matching tactic for controlling where locals are allocated and when stack storage is considered live.
If a formerly local function needs to be linked/exported, update the header and forward include path at the same time as the matching change.
Marking a file Matching in `configure.py` should be backed by decomp.dev/report evidence; here the bot confirmed the only remaining function reached 100.00%.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files use the `ftCo_` prefix, e.g. `ftCo_BarrelWait.c`, `ftCo_BarrelWait_IASA`, `ftCo_BarrelWait_Phys`, and `ftCo_BarrelWait_Coll`.
Unknown-address functions retain address-style names such as `ftCo_8009EB18`, `ftCo_8009EC44`, and `ftCo_8009EC70`.
Temporary or fake layout helpers should be explicitly named and documented; this PR used `struct SmallerHitCapsule` with a `@todo Fake` comment.
Damage subfields remain address/offset-style where not yet semantically named, e.g. `x1848_kb_angle`, `x184c_damaged_hurtbox`, `x1854_collpos`, and `x1860_element`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced dummy stack arrays with `PAD_STACK(4)` in two functions to preserve stack shape without inventing real variables.
Used a fake smaller stack struct for hit-capsule data and cast it to `HitCapsule*` at API boundaries.
Introduced nested blocks around `Fighter* fp` and `struct SmallerHitCapsule hit` in `ftCo_8009EC70`, likely to influence stack allocation/order.
Used explicit `0.0f`, `1.0f`, and `180.0f` float literals and `fp->facing_dir < 0.0f` to better match floating-point code generation.
Kept the knockback-angle adjustment as `if (kb_angle < 0) { kb_angle += 360; }` followed by facing-direction mirroring, preserving the original control flow while improving type precision.
Bot evidence showed `ftCo_8009EC70` improved from 99.94% to 100.00%, enabling the whole object to be linked.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 progress: matched code 38.96% (+0.01%, +308 bytes), linked code 25.89% (+0.02%, +652 bytes), and one new match for `main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_BarrelWait` / `ftCo_8009EC70`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace `SmallerHitCapsule` with the real `HitCapsule` size/layout.
Search for other uses of `PAD_STACK` replacing dummy `u8 _[]` arrays in fighter common action files.
Look for other near-matching functions blocked by stack-local collision or hit-capsule struct layout.
Audit headers that still include `ftCommon/forward.h` where `ft/forward.h` may be the correct broader forward declaration source.
Search for subsequent renames of `ftCo_8009EC70` or damage fields such as `x1848_kb_angle` and `x1860_element`.
